summary: Healthier lifestyles drastically decrease the prevalence of chronic diseases—the largest driver of healthcare expenditures—which leads to reduced use of diagnostics, medications, hospitalizations, and emergency services. This preventive effect results in overall lower healthcare resource utilization over time.
